source_jobs.title: Product Marketing Specialist
source_jobs.description:
The Role

As a Product Marketing Specialist for Membership, you’ll own the strategy and execution of direct sales marketing funnels that drive revenue and membership growth. You’ll blend data-driven decision making with a deep understanding of human psychology to create campaigns that attract, engage, and convert audiences—delivering measurable results and increasing customer lifetime value.

What you'll do

 - Design and optimize direct sales marketing strategies to hit revenue and performance targets

 - Collaborate with content creators to develop compelling marketing collateral and sales materials

 - Plan, execute, and manage multi-channel campaigns (email, social, paid ads) to grow and convert the top of the funnel

 - Analyze funnel and campaign performance, translating data into actionable insights for continuous improvement

 - Refine audience segmentation and personalization to boost conversion rates

 - Partner closely with the Membership Business Unit to align marketing with business goals

 - Monitor customer feedback and sentiment to inform campaign adjustments


What success looks like

 - Launched and optimized at least two direct sales campaigns, achieving or exceeding ROAS targets within 90 days

 - Demonstrated measurable top-of-funnel growth, with increased qualified leads and improved conversion rates

 - Utilized campaign insights and data to drive clear improvements in messaging, targeting, and revenue outcomes

 - Collaborated effectively across teams and served as a trusted partner to stakeholders
   

What we're looking for

 - 3+ years’ experience in product marketing, campaign management, or a related field

 - Proven track record of planning and executing successful direct sales marketing campaigns

 - Strong understanding of funnel marketing, human psychology, and content that converts

 - Proficiency with marketing analytics tools and data-driven decision making

 - Excellent communication and project management skills

 - Demonstrated use of AI tools in marketing or a strong baseline of AI proficiency
   
   Nice-to-haves:

 - Experience with membership or subscription-based products

 - Familiarity with paid advertising platforms and advanced segmentation strategies

 - Bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Business, or related field


Mindvalley is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, colour, religion, gender identity or expression, national origin, age, disability, marital status, sexual orientation, or any other legally protected status. We are committed to creating a diverse and inclusive workplace and encourage applications from all qualified individuals.

source_jobs.title: Media Buyer
source_jobs.description:
About the Role:

As a Media Buyer at Mindvalley, you’ll drive the planning, execution, and optimization of digital advertising campaigns that fuel our global growth. You’ll own campaign performance, leverage AI-driven solutions, and collaborate closely with creative and funnel teams to deliver high-impact results across multiple platforms.


What you’ll do:

 - Launch, manage, and scale paid social campaigns across Meta, Google, and TikTok, focusing on new customer acquisition via VSL and webinar funnels

 - Propose and execute structured A/B tests on hooks, copy, creatives, audiences, and campaign structures

 - Monitor and optimize key metrics (CTR, CPM, CVR, CPA, ROAS) to inform scaling decisions

 - Collaborate with creative, funnel, and product teams to align messaging and creative direction

 - Translate funnel objectives into actionable creative briefs and performance-driven ad concepts

 - Ensure accurate tracking and attribution across pixels, Conversions API, and GA4

 - Maintain campaign dashboards and deliver weekly performance insights and recommendations

 - Act as a feedback engine for creative teams, identifying winning angles and escalating learnings

 - Stay ahead of platform trends, best practices, and new ad features to keep Mindvalley’s strategy cutting-edge



What success looks like

 - Campaigns are launched and optimized with clear, measurable improvements in ROAS and lead acquisition

 - Structured testing delivers actionable insights, with at least two new winning creative angles identified

 - Accurate tracking and attribution are in place, with reliable reporting on all key metrics

 - Creative and funnel teams receive regular, data-driven feedback that improves ad performance

 - Mindvalley’s ad strategy is benchmarked and iterated to match or exceed industry leaders
   

What we’re looking for

 - 4+ years of hands-on experience managing paid social campaigns (Meta, YouTube, TikTok) with proven results

 - Deep understanding of platform-specific best practices, campaign structures, and creative formats

 - Strong analytical skills and proficiency with data analytics and AI-driven tools for campaign optimization

 - Relentless approach to testing and experimentation across creatives, audiences, and bidding strategies

 - Experience collaborating with creative and marketing teams to develop high-performing ads and funnels

 - Nice-to-have: Experience with advanced tracking setups (Conversions API, GA4), and a portfolio of AI-driven campaign improvements


Mindvalley is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, colour, religion, gender identity or expression, national origin, age, disability, marital status, sexual orientation, or any other legally protected status. We are committed to creating a diverse and inclusive workplace and encourage applications from all qualified individuals.

source_jobs.title: Affiliate Manager
source_jobs.description:
The Role

As an Affiliate Manager, you’ll drive Mindvalley’s growth by building and nurturing strategic affiliate partnerships. You’ll recruit, onboard, and empower affiliates to promote our transformational programs, expanding our reach and impact globally. Your work will directly shape how new audiences discover Mindvalley.


What You'll do:

 - Design and execute a comprehensive affiliate marketing strategy to grow revenue and customer acquisition

 - Recruit, onboard, and nurture affiliates across diverse categories and channels, including websites, newsletters, and authors

 - Negotiate and secure mutually beneficial terms with affiliates and direct partners

 - Continuously analyze and optimize affiliate performance to maximize ROI

 - Develop and oversee bespoke partnership deals beyond traditional affiliate networks

 - Provide resources, marketing collateral, and ongoing support to partners

 - Represent Mindvalley at industry events, building strong relationships with key affiliates and partners

 - Monitor market trends, competitor campaigns, and leverage SEO, media buying, and sales funnel expertise to enhance program effectiveness



What success looks like:

 - 90 days in, you’ve onboarded new high-potential affiliates and partners, with clear growth in affiliate-driven revenue

 - Affiliate program performance is tracked, with actionable insights and optimizations already implemented

 - Key relationships are established, and Mindvalley’s presence is growing across new channels and audiences


Experience:

 - Proven track record of growing and managing successful affiliate programs

 - Strong understanding of SEO, media buying, funnels, and marketing strategies

 - Excellent communication, networking, and negotiation skills

 - Data-driven mindset with experience in performance analysis

 - Entrepreneurial, proactive, and independent approach

 - Experience with affiliate platforms and tools



Mindvalley is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, colour, religion, gender identity or expression, national origin, age, disability, marital status, sexual orientation, or any other legally protected status. We are committed to creating a diverse and inclusive workplace and encourage applications from all qualified individuals.

source_jobs.title: SEO Lead
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT THE ROLE

As SEO Lead, you’ll drive Mindvalley’s organic growth by shaping how our brand is discovered in a world where AI-driven search is the new standard. You’ll lead the evolution from traditional SEO to Generative Engine Optimization (GEO), ensuring our content is visible, cited, and trusted in AI-powered search results. Your work will directly impact how millions find and engage with Mindvalley’s transformational content.




WHAT YOU'LL DO:

 - Develop and execute a forward-thinking SEO strategy focused on AI visibility, citability, and brand authority

 - Structure and optimize content for AI-driven search, ensuring factual, concise, and machine-readable information

 - Own and track organic growth metrics—visibility, engagement, lead generation, and revenue

 - Collaborate with content, social, email, and PR teams to align messaging and maximize organic reach

 - Lead on-page and technical SEO improvements, staying ahead of search trends and algorithm changes

 - Mentor and guide a team of SEO and analytics specialists, fostering collaboration and continuous learning

 - Explore and test emerging channels and platforms to expand Mindvalley’s organic footprint
   
   


WHAT SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE:

 - Mindvalley’s content is consistently cited in AI-generated search results and overviews

 - Organic visibility and engagement metrics show measurable growth across key channels

 - The team delivers structured, AI-friendly content that drives brand authority and trust

 - Clear, actionable reporting on organic performance is established and shared across teams
   
   


WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR?

 - Proven experience leading SEO strategies in fast-evolving digital environments

 - Deep understanding of AI-driven search, structured data, and entity-based optimization

 - Strong analytical skills and a data-driven approach to decision-making

 - Experience managing and mentoring teams

 - Excellent collaboration and communication skills

 - Experience with AI tools for SEO, background in content or PR, familiarity with emerging digital platforms
   

Mindvalley is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, colour, religion, gender identity or expression, national origin, age, disability, marital status, sexual orientation, or any other legally protected status. We are committed to creating a diverse and inclusive workplace and encourage applications from all qualified individuals.

source_jobs.title: Social Media Manager
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT THE ROLE


As Social Media Manager, you’ll lead and elevate Mindvalley’s social media presence across all platforms, driving engagement, growth, and brand consistency. You’ll shape and execute strategy, manage a talented team, and ensure our content inspires and connects with a global audience.




WHAT YOU'LL DO:

 - Improve and execute Mindvalley’s social media strategy in partnership with the Chief Growth Officer

 - Oversee, brainstorm, and manage all content published on social channels

 - Measure and report on campaign performance using the right metrics and analytics tools

 - Stay ahead of social media trends and quickly implement new best practices

 - Use and optimize tools like Agorapulse; introduce automation to streamline team workflows

 - Collaborate with content strategists, campaign managers, copywriters, designers, and cross-functional teams

 - Build and nurture relationships with industry professionals and influencers

 - Continuously optimize every checkpoint on each platform for maximum impact
   
   


WHAT SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE:

 - Social media strategy is refined and delivering measurable growth in reach, engagement, and leads

 - Content output is consistent, high-quality, and fully aligned with Mindvalley’s brand

 - Team members are clear on goals, empowered, and performing at a high level

 - New trends and tools are identified and implemented, keeping Mindvalley ahead of the curve

 - Strong cross-team collaboration and influencer partnerships are established
   

Experience:

 - 5+ years in social media management, content strategy, or digital marketing

 - 2+ years leading a social media team

 - Proven results growing social channels for major brands

 - Deep knowledge of Facebook, YouTube, Twitter, LinkedIn, Instagram, TikTok, and emerging platforms

 - Mastery of social media algorithms, metrics, and optimization for reach, engagement, and revenue

 - Experience with audience and persona research

 - Strong project management, time management, and communication skills

 - Problem-solving mindset and attention to detail

 - Bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Communications, or related field
   

Mindvalley is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, colour, religion, gender identity or expression, national origin, age, disability, marital status, sexual orientation, or any other legally protected status. We are committed to creating a diverse and inclusive workplace and encourage applications from all qualified individuals.

notes: No tech roles, mostly marketing and sales positions
